title: Am I wasting too much time, ebullshitring more than I need to
html:
Am I wasting too much time, ebullshitring more than I need to

Source: 2024-01-28 Apple Note. Title-only entry — body is empty. The title itself is the artifact: "Am I wasting too much time. Ebullshitring more than I need too." A single uncomposed thought from late-OSU senior-spring, frozen mid-typo.

TL;DR

  • Body is empty — only the title exists. The act of opening Notes and typing this title was the entire intervention.
  • "Ebullshitring" — neologism, probably a typo merger of "e-bullshit" + "ringing"/"-ering" (the phone-rings-and-you-pick-up energy of compulsive scrolling).
  • Captured 7:43 AM — the early-morning self-doubt window, before the day-shape forms.
  • Same flavor as the "wasting time" undertone in the March 2024 four domain scaffold (also full of empty quadrants) and May 2024's biggest lesson note.
  • The unwritten body is the whole signal: he asked the question and didn't follow through, which is exactly the behavior the title is naming.

Pattern

A small but real category in his corpus: title-as-entire-note. The opening of Notes is itself the discharge mechanism — once the question lands in text he doesn't always need to develop it. Same shape as this one and a handful of other one-liner panic-titles in early 2024.

Open questions

  • What was the specific stimulus that morning — a missed deadline, a YouTube binge, a Twitter spiral?
  • Did this question recur, or did the May 2024 Asia trip resolve it by force-changing the environment?

  • shanghai study abroad
